message:
fix(rch): Improve checksum extraction for single-hash-only files

The extract_checksum() function failed to correctly handle checksum files
that contain only a hash on a single line (common format from GitHub
release assets and other tools).

Previous logic checked `!content.contains(char::is_whitespace)` which
failed for files ending with a newline character, causing valid checksums
to be rejected with "Checksum not found" errors.

Changes:
- Pre-filter empty lines before processing
- Check if file contains exactly one non-empty line with a single token
- Return that token directly as the checksum (no filename matching needed)
- Update test to use realistic checksum file content (with trailing newline)

This fixes self-update failures when checksum files use the minimal
hash-only format rather than the traditional "hash filename" format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
